description: Returns a component of the page URL. Exactly three modes: (1) a specific GET parameter value when value is 'parameter' and options.parameter_name is set; (2) the full query string when value is 'query'; (3) the protocol (http/https) when value is 'protocol'. Missing or empty GET params return empty string; numeric and TRUE/FALSE strings are converted to numbers/booleans.

when_to_use: Read UTM or campaign parameters (utm_source, utm_medium, source, etc.) — use value 'parameter' and options.parameter_name
Get the full query string for logging or conditions — use value 'query'
Detect protocol (http vs https) for mixed content or security logic — use value 'protocol'
common_use_cases: Full query: value 'query' returns the complete query string
best_practices: Use value 'parameter' only with options.parameter_name set to the GET parameter name
value must be exactly one of: parameter, query, protocol
When parameter is absent or empty, variable value is empty string

create_variable.description: Create a URL variable. Set value to one of: parameter, query, protocol. When value is 'parameter', set options.parameter_name to the GET parameter name.

value.description: One of exactly 3 values (API: value): 'parameter', 'query', or 'protocol'.

options.description: When value is 'parameter', required: { "parameter_name": "<GET param name>" } e.g. { "parameter_name": "source" }. Omit or leave empty for 'query' and 'protocol'.

value.description: Which URL part to return (API: value). Exactly one of: 'parameter', 'query', 'protocol'.
value.recommendation: Use 'parameter' for a single GET parameter (then set options.parameter_name). Use 'query' for the full query string, 'protocol' for http/https.

options.description: Template options. When value is 'parameter', required: parameter_name (the GET parameter name, e.g. source, utm_medium).

parameter_name.description: Name of the GET parameter to read (e.g. source, utm_source). Required when value is 'parameter'.

options.recommendation: When value is 'parameter', always set options.parameter_name to the query parameter name. Case-sensitive.

create_parameter_example.description: Create a URL variable for the source GET parameter (value 'parameter' + options.parameter_name)

update_parameter_example.description: Update URL variable to a different parameter name

query_example.description: URL variable for full query string (value 'query', no options)

protocol_example.description: URL variable for protocol (http or https)

common_mistakes: Using a GET parameter name as value instead of value 'parameter' plus options.parameter_name
Using any value other than 'parameter', 'query', or 'protocol'
Expecting a value when the GET parameter is missing—variable returns empty string

solutions: For value 'parameter': confirm the page URL includes the query parameter and options.parameter_name matches it (case-sensitive)
For value 'query': ensure the URL has a query string; otherwise value is empty
Check that value is exactly one of: parameter, query, protocol
